title: Signal Coverage Forecast
description: Forecast-shaped availability guidance for a signal. Use this when a seller or signal source can expose how much of a declared inventory denominator has a signal present, absent, or present with specific values. This object intentionally omits DeliveryForecast.currency because signal coverage can be unitless.

enclosing_signal_identity_match.description: When SignalCoverageForecast is nested under a get_signals signal item or wholesale signal object, every point's signal dimension MUST resolve to the enclosing signal identity. If a point uses signal_ref, compare it to the enclosing signal_ref when present. If it uses signal_id shorthand, compare it to the enclosing signal_ref.signal_id or legacy signal_id.id. Mismatches are conformance failures.

complete_exclusive_partition.description: When bucket_completeness is 'complete' and bucket_semantics is 'exclusive', returned points are declared to cover the full denominator. Conformance tooling SHOULD verify coverage_rate mid values sum to 1.0 when all points provide mid values, and SHOULD apply equivalent low/high partition checks when ranges are supplied.

points.description: Coverage or availability points. Each point reuses the standard ForecastPoint shape, MUST include a signal dimension, and MUST include metrics.coverage_rate. Use metrics.impressions for count denominators and metrics.coverage_rate for the fraction of the declared scope represented by the point.

forecast_range_unit.description: How to interpret the points array. Signal coverage forecasts always use 'availability' because the points describe available inventory or population coverage, not spend curves or temporal pacing.

method.description: Method used to produce this coverage forecast.

scope.description: Explicit denominator for the coverage forecast. This identifies the inventory, product, account, or custom universe that coverage_rate values are relative to. Additional seller-specific qualifiers are allowed for scopes such as line item type, ad server, inventory class, country, or flight window.

kind.description: Denominator family for the coverage forecast.

label.description: Human-readable denominator label, such as 'network price-priority inventory'.

countries.description: Countries included in the denominator, as ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes.

line_item_types.description: Seller or ad-server line item types included in the denominator.

date_range.description: Historical or planned date window used to compute the denominator.

bucket_semantics.description: 'exclusive' means the returned signal-value buckets do not overlap with each other. 'overlapping' means one impression or user can appear in multiple returned buckets, so coverage_rate values may sum above 1.0. This field describes overlap among returned buckets; bucket_completeness declares whether the returned buckets cover the full denominator.

bucket_completeness.description: 'complete' means the returned buckets cover the declared denominator. For complete + exclusive forecasts, count metrics and coverage_rate values can be treated as a full partition, subject to metric additivity rules. 'partial' means omitted denominator share represents undisclosed, other, or unsupported buckets; buyers MUST NOT infer totals by summing returned points.

generated_at.description: When this coverage forecast was computed.
